_meta.description: Custom technology fingerprints loaded as an overlay on top of the upstream Wappalyzer database. Adds detections for CMS platforms, hospitality and tourism booking engines, accommodation PMS, channel managers, CSS frameworks, and POS systems where the upstream fingerprints are insufficient or missing.
_meta.notes: Do not edit the a-z.json files (vendored from enthec/webappanalyzer). Add custom fingerprints here instead.

_disabled_meta.description: Techs explicitly removed from the loaded DB after overlay merge. These upstream Wappalyzer fingerprints have well-known false-positive issues that no amount of pattern-tightening fixes (their HTML selectors match too-generic markup). Validated against tests/bench_tech_detect.py: disabling this list raises corpus precision from 0.621 -> ~0.9+ with no recall loss on the curated 10-site corpus.
_disabled_meta.Element UI: Matches generic 'el-' class prefixes used by countless unrelated component libraries. Fires on Stripe, Shopify, GitHub, Squarespace, etc.
_disabled_meta.Cart Functionality: Loose regex on cart-shaped patterns; fires on any site mentioning 'cart' or 'checkout' in marketing copy.
_disabled_meta.Google Sites: Pattern matches generic Google CSS - fires on any site that pulls a Google font or analytics asset.
_disabled_meta.C3.js: Generic chart-library pattern; fires on sites using unrelated charting code or with 'c3' in class names.
_disabled_meta.Contentful: Overbroad - fires on sites referencing the word 'contentful' or shipping similar SDK-style class names.
_disabled_meta.ZURB Foundation: Loose class-name match; fires on sites with unrelated 'foundation' word usage. Real Foundation usage has dropped sharply post-2020.

_added_meta.description: Overlay patches for upstream fingerprints that are outdated or missing. Each entry below extends or replaces a flawed upstream pattern.
_added_meta.GSAP: Upstream pattern only matches 'TweenMax.min.js' (GSAP 2.x). Modern GSAP 3.x ships as 'gsap.min.js' + plugin files (ScrollTrigger, SplitText, ...). The added patterns catch the 3.x shape.
_added_meta.Amazon CloudFront: Upstream entry exists but has zero detection patterns (scriptSrc=None, script=None, url=None). CloudFront is normally inferred from DNS CNAMEs which we can't see. Add a scriptSrc match on '*.cloudfront.net' which catches sites loading assets through CloudFront.
_added_meta.Node.js: Upstream entry has zero detection patterns AND no upstream tech implies it. Result: Node.js never fires, even when a Node-only framework (Next.js, Nuxt.js, Astro, Express) is detected. Add direct header patterns + extend Next.js / Nuxt.js / Vercel implies chains so any of those infer Node.js automatically.
_added_meta.Next.js implies Node.js: Upstream implies = [React, Webpack]. Next.js is a Node.js framework by definition - add Node.js to the implies list.
_added_meta.Nuxt.js implies Node.js: Same shape as Next.js for Vue. Already implies Vue.js upstream; add Node.js too.
_added_meta.Vercel implies Node.js: Vercel's serverless runtime is Node.js. Detecting Vercel reliably implies Node.js at build/runtime.
_added_meta.Loom: Upstream entry has zero patterns. Common embed surface for marketing/sales pages. Match script src + iframe to loom.com/(share|embed)/.
_added_meta.DocuSign: Upstream entry has zero patterns. Match script src on js.docusign.com / *.docusign.net plus iframe embeds.
_added_meta.Dropbox: Upstream entry has zero patterns. Match scriptSrc to www.dropbox.com/static/api or *.dropboxstatic.com, and Saver/Chooser button classes.
_added_meta.Index Exchange: Upstream entry has zero patterns. Match scriptSrc to indexww.com (their bid CDN).
_added_meta.Sitecore Experience Platform: Upstream entry has zero patterns. Match the canonical SC_ANALYTICS_GLOBAL_COOKIE plus X-Powered-By header plus <meta generator='Sitecore'> when present.
_added_meta.Triple Whale: Upstream entry has zero patterns. Match scriptSrc to triplewhale.com / triplewhale-pixel.com.
Audit 2026-06-01: 10 JS frameworks ship without Node.js in their upstream implies chain (Astro, Remix, SvelteKit, SolidStart, Gatsby, Strapi, Eleventy, Gridsome, VuePress, VitePress). All are Node.js build tools; even when they output static HTML, the build is Node-driven. Patched.
_added_meta.Implies chain - PHP frameworks need PHP: Zend and WooCommerce both ship without PHP in implies. WooCommerce is especially broken - it's a WordPress plugin and should imply WordPress + PHP + MySQL. Patched.
_added_meta.Implies chain - non-JS/non-PHP language: Audit 2026-06-02: swept Python/Ruby/Rust/Go/Java/.NET/Elixir/Crystal/Erlang frameworks for missing language implies. Patched 9 broken chains: Amber/Kemal (Crystal), Streamlit/PyWebIO/CherryPy (Python), WEBrick (Ruby), Yaws (Erlang), Hugo (Go), Turbopack (Rust). Most curated language ecosystems (Spring->Java, Rails->Ruby, Phoenix Framework->Elixir, Revel->Go, Django/Flask/FastAPI->Python) already had correct upstream chains. Skipped Bottle (upstream entry is meal-delivery SaaS, not Python framework), Sinatra (upstream entry is WordPress theme), Phoenix (upstream entry is a Node CMS, not Elixir Phoenix), Warp (Haskell Warp is correct as-is), Play (already implies Scala, Java optional).
_added_meta.Categorise hospitality overlay (2026-06-: Audit found 6 custom-overlay techs firing without `cats` (categories=[] in tech-detect output, silently breaking --only-categories/--exclude-categories filtering). Added cats to Localis, Simpleview CMS, Tock, TheFork, Eventbrite (truly uncategorised). Extended cats on ATDW, FareHarbor, Rezdy, Bokun, Mews, Cloudbeds, SiteMinder, Triptease, SevenRooms where upstream categorisation was incomplete for what the platforms actually are. Regression guard: test_custom_overlay_categories_are_set.
_added_meta.Roam: Tourism content platform powering ~20 destination-marketing sites (melbournenow, visitphillipisland, visitgeelongbellarine, ...). Detected purely via the `X-ROAM` response header set by Roam's edge cache. Header is unique to Roam — no other vendor uses it.
_added_meta.Consolidate duplicate ATDW/Bokun entries: custom_fingerprints.json had duplicate top-level keys for ATDW and Bokun. Python's json.loads silently keeps only the last duplicate, so the first entry's detection patterns were dead code. Consolidated each pair into a single entry, preserving the union of scriptSrc/html/dom patterns from both.
